skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic-inspired brand colors, typography guidance, and visual styling rules to artifacts that need a consistent brand presentation.
skill.summary: Guides artifacts toward consistent Anthropic-style colors, typography, and visual hierarchy.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs, weak cryptography, command execution, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Apache License text, Markdown inline code around color values, and ordinary brand-guideline wording. No executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License URLs Are Static Legal References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs appear only in the Apache License text. They are not used by executable code and do not initiate network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are standard Apache License references in a license file. No code path reads or executes them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Apache License Text Is Not Cryptographic Code
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to legal wording in the Apache License. The file contains no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are license prose about contributions, notices, trademarks, warranties, and file descriptions. There is no executable cryptographic logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Color Values Are Not Shell Commands
low_findings.description: The backtick alerts are Markdown inline code spans around color values. They do not instruct the assistant to execute shell, Ruby, or system commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each backtick wraps a literal hex color in documentation. No command name, interpolation, or execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Brand Wording Is Not Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts in the skill file are triggered by ordinary words in brand and design documentation. No crypto API or algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe brand colors, typography, visual design, and font fallback behavior. There is no evidence of encryption, hashing, or password handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Documentation Is Not Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to benign styling terms and a python-pptx color class reference. They do not collect host, network, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines describe a gray color and RGBColor usage for styling. No scanning, probing, enumeration, or exfiltration instruction appears.

content.value_statement: Teams often need consistent brand presentation across documents and slides. This skill provides practical color, typography, and styling guidance for Anthropic-style artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Lists primary and accent colors for Anthropic-style visual work.
Specifies heading and body font choices with fallback fonts.
Guides text hierarchy for headings, body text, and secondary elements.
Suggests accent color use for non-text shapes and visual elements.
Explains how to preserve readability when custom fonts are unavailable.
limitations: Does not include executable tools for editing files automatically.
Does not install Poppins, Lora, or other fonts.
Does not verify permission to use Anthropic branding.
Does not provide complete corporate identity rules beyond the listed colors and fonts.

use_cases.title: Style a Presentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, heading fonts, body fonts, and accent shapes to a slide deck.

use_cases.title: Format a Report
use_cases.description: Turn a plain written artifact into a more consistent branded document with readable hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Review Visual Consistency
use_cases.description: Check whether an artifact follows the provided palette, typography, and readability guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple document or slide update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brand-guidelines skill to style this artifact with the listed colors, heading font, body font, and readable text contrast.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Brand Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for an existing visual artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this artifact and apply a brand pass. Update colors, typography, text hierarchy, and accent elements using the skill guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Design Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for review without direct editing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this artifact against the brand-guidelines skill. Report mismatched colors, weak typography, poor contrast, and inconsistent accent usage.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Implementation Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for handoff to a design or engineering team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the brand-guidelines skill into implementation guidance for this project. Include color roles, font roles, fallback behavior, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: A slide deck uses mixed fonts, several unrelated colors, and low contrast captions.
output: Headings use Poppins with Arial fallback.
Body text uses Lora with Georgia fallback.
Primary text uses the dark color on light backgrounds.
Accent shapes rotate through orange, blue, and green.

output_examples.input: A product brief needs a quick visual consistency review.
output_examples.output: The review identifies off-palette colors, inconsistent heading styles, and places where contrast should be improved before publication.

best_practices: Use the palette roles consistently instead of introducing similar extra colors.
Check contrast after applying brand colors to text and backgrounds.
Install the recommended fonts when exact typography matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as proof of legal permission to use a brand.
Do not apply accent colors to every element without hierarchy.
Do not rely on custom fonts without verifying fallback rendering.

faq.question: Does this skill edit files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides styling guidance that an assistant or user can apply to artifacts.

faq.question: Which fonts does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends Poppins for headings and Lora for body text, with Arial and Georgia as fallbacks.

faq.question: Can I use it for slide decks?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is suitable for presentations, reports, and other visual artifacts.

faq.question: Does it install fonts?
faq.answer: No. Fonts must already be installed when exact font rendering is required.

faq.question: Is this an official Anthropic publication?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the reviewed files that this community skill is an official Anthropic publication.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing branded work?
faq.answer: Confirm brand usage permission, color contrast, font availability, and consistency across the final artifact.
